title: Ways to Climb a Staircase
description:
Given You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can climb either 1 or 2 steps. Return the number of distinct ways to reach the top. Use integer arithmetic.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 45
explanation:
Intuition
This is the Fibonacci progression where f(n)=f(n-1)+f(n-2) because the last move is either a 1-step from n-1 or a 2-step from n-2. Iterative DP accumulates answers while keeping only the last two values.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Let a=f(0)=1, b=f(1)=1
2. Main Algorithm: Iterate i from 2..n, update a,b to next fib
3. Return Result: Return b as f(n)

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(1)

Key Insights
- Iterative rolling variables avoid arrays
- DP base f(0)=1, f(1)=1

Common Pitfalls
- Off-by-one when initializing
- Overflow not an issue within constraints
hints: Define f(n) using the last step
